findings.title: Suspicious Executable: install_flash_player.exe
findings.description: Potentially malicious installer detected. Flash Player installers are commonly used as delivery vectors for malware and PUPs. This file warrants immediate hash analysis and behavioral investigation.

findings.title: Multiple Suspicious Windows Installer Executables
findings.description: Three Windows Installer-related executables detected (instmsia.exe, instmsiw.exe, InstMsi.exe). These are commonly associated with malware droppers and maintenance tools used in post-compromise activi

findings.title: Suspicious Media Application File: AppCurrentItemPlaying.sol
findings.description: Suspicious .sol file related to media playback. This file type is sometimes used for obfuscation or persistence mechanisms by malware.

findings.title: Suspicious Temp Directory
findings.description: Temp folder flagged as suspicious. This is a common location for malware staging, execution, and temporary payload storage.

three_claim_trace.claim: Flash Player installer detected as suspicious executable in Temp directory
three_claim_trace.evidence: install_flash_player.exe flagged by SleuthKit fls — file type r/r, extension .exe, path matches suspicious_paths pattern. SHA-256 verified before and after tool execution.
three_claim_trace.verdict: SUPPORTED — tool output contains install_flash_player.exe in fls results

three_claim_trace.claim: MSMSGS found in HKCU Run registry key via NTUSER.DAT extraction
three_claim_trace.evidence: NTUSER.DAT hive extracted via icat, parsed by regipy — key path \Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run, value name MSMSGS, value data C:\Program Files\Messenger\msmsgs.exe /background
three_claim_trace.verdict: SUPPORTED — registry hive extraction + regipy parse confirms Run key entry

three_claim_trace.claim: .sol file classification corrected by Verifier — Timeline misidentified as suspicious, Verifier identified as Adobe Flash LSO
three_claim_trace.evidence: Timeline Agent flagged AppCurrentItemPlaying.sol as suspicious media file. Verifier cross-referenced with Flash Player installer finding and system context, determined .sol is Adobe Flash Local Shared Object — benign artifact consistent with Flash installation. Self-correction logged with message_type='correction' and self_correction=true in agent_messages.
three_claim_trace.verdict: SUPPORTED — Verifier contradiction documents the misclassification; agent_messages trace shows self_correction: true

lowest_confidence_areas: all timeline findings are single-source (no memory corroboration for disk-only images)
registry parsing limited to NTUSER.DAT Run/RunOnce — SYSTEM and SOFTWARE hives not covered
known_limitations: Memory Agent requires RAM capture (.vmem/.mem) — returns honest zero for disk-only images
Timeline uses SleuthKit fls — Plaso not available in this container build
Registry parsing via regipy covers NTUSER.DAT only; SYSTEM/SOFTWARE hives not parsed
No Prefetch, AmCache, or ShimCache analysis in current build
